pointers.Insert_signature: engine/btree/insert.go: func Insert(store *NodeStore, alloc *NodeAllocator, rootNodeID uint64, path string, fileID uint64) (newRootNodeID uint64, err error)
pointers.NodeAllocator: engine/btree/insert.go: type NodeAllocator (sidecar file <index-path>.nodealloc, pattern mirrors engine/catalog/idalloc.go)
pointers.propagation: engine/btree/insert.go: propagateSplit (handles ancestor overflow chain and new-root allocation)
pointers.shared_descent: engine/btree/lookup.go: descendToLeaf (used by both Lookup and Insert)
pointers.tests: engine/btree/insert_test.go: TestInsertEmptyTree, TestInsertUpsert, TestInsertLeafSplit, TestInsertInternalSplit, TestInsertOutOfOrder

known_gaps_for_downstream: No persistence yet of 'current root node ID' across process restarts -- caller must track newRootNodeID returned by Insert. Flagged for 1.2.5 (prefix scan) / 1.2.6 (persist/reload).

NodeAllocator's high-water-mark sidecar file (<index-path>.nodealloc) is durable per-index-file but not yet wired into any single top-level 'open tree' API -- there is no such API yet in this subtask's scope.

Version field on nodes is round-tripped but not bumped by Insert; concurrency (latch-crabbing, optimistic reads) explicitly deferred to a later concurrency-focused subtask, consistent with 1.2.1/1.2.2's existing treatment.
closed_findings: 1.2.2 verification gap: internal nodes with >= 2 separator keys were never exercised by Lookup (buildTestTree scaffolding only built single-key internal nodes). Closed by TestInsertInternalSplit, which explicitly asserts such a node exists post-insert and runs full-tree Lookup against it.
self_consistency: .cdr/runs/2026-07-04/023-implementation/self-consistency.json (build/vet/test all PASS; internal sanity only, NOT independent verification per invariant I4)
next_step: Hand off to /cdr:verify for independent verification of task-1.2.3.
